publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-10-27 18:32:47 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, photographs, technical reports and reprints, drafts of articles, manuscripts, and subject files.
did.abstract: Correspondence, photographs, technical reports and reprints, drafts of articles, manuscripts, and subject files pertaining to Ewald's career as a scientist working in the field of x-ray crystallography; also, personal and family papers.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Scientist, crystallographer; professor, University of Munich, Queen's University at Belfast, and the Polytechnic Institute of Brooklyn. Professionally known as P. P. Ewald; personally known as Paul P. Ewald.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, photographs, technical reports and reprints, drafts of articles, manuscripts, and subject files pertaining to Ewald's career as a scientist working in the field of x-ray crystallography; also, personal and family papers.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Paul P. Ewald papers, #4586. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: The Crystallography of Human Stones - Acknowledgements, Chs. 1-2

The Crystallography of Human Stones - Chs. 7-8, Bibliography, etc.
Borrmann, Gerhard from Dr. G. Hildebrandt
The Crystallography of Human Stones - Figures
The Crystallography of Human Stones - Plates I-V
The Crystallography of Human Stones - Lonsdale and Sutor
Hsum Hu, S. R. Goodman - Pole Figures of Cu
Bragg Obituary for Physics Today mss.
Festschrift Y. Cauchois - "Correction" of Bragg Law, 1-29-79
E. E. Symposia - P.I.B.-MRI - Solid State
Hans Bethe - Seattle Summer - Lecture and Seminar Notes
Overlap in Diamond; Electrical Properties of Thin Metallic Films; Potentials of Crystals and Half Crystals
Juretschke - Application of Group Theory and Physical Properties of Crystala
Hydro-Electrodyn Optics, Belfast - Hydrodynamics Courses II and III
Hydro-Electrodyn Optics, Belfast - III, 1st Term: Electromagnetic Theory
Hydro-Electrodyn Optics, Belfast - V, Optics; Future Development of the Department of Math. Physics, Queensland U.
Guggenheim - R. S. "Obituary" of P. P. E.French Life Dates
Article: Carl Hermann for Dictionary of Scientific Biography
Two samples of Acta. Cryst. correspondence
J. D. H. Donnay translation of 1921 paper by Ewald
Notes on Slater,....for Coloquia on Irregular Crystal
History of Science Correspondence: Beyerchen, Olby, Weiner
Center for History of Recent Physics - Niels Bohr Library
History of Quantum Physics including Born interview by Ewald
Sources for History of Quantum Physics
Sources for History of Quantum Physics - T. S. Juhn Archives
Strukturbericht Correspondence - Johnson Reprints, ACA, Oosthock
L. V. Azaroff on X-Ray Physics
Correspondence - IV Cryst, M. P. Gesellschaft
Talks: Parma Institute; IBM talk - "60 Years of Crystal Optics"
Comments on Inverse Pole Figure Methods
Solid State Physics - Div. of Amer. Physical Soc.
U. S. National Committee for Crystallography - Tamiment
Tommasi, Dr. G. B. de Vignano
ACA - Bond Printing and Publishing Service
ACA - Evans, Dr. Howard T. J.
ACA - Membership (dues, reminder for payment, etc.)
American Physics Society; NYSSAPS Fall Meeting
P146 and P147 Dasgupta and Maitra, Basak and Dasgupta
E1029 - 1732 S. Chandzasekhaz - A Simple Model for Optical Activity [empty]; "Refused - back to Evans[?], 1 Dec. 1955"
F578 Eichhorn - On the Use of Gen. Inclinations
E475-803 Quarashi and Vand; P132 Quarashi
"A Review of My Papers on Crystal Optics 1912 to 1968" by Ewald
Cambridge Talk - Introduction to Dynamical Theory
Correspondence - Forman and Co.-Ewald in preparation of Myth of Myths
Interview - part of Weiner's interview in N. Milford
Reprints and Articles by Ewald and others
Correspondence re Inauguration of Structure Reports; International Tables; Bragg, Bernal, Vergard, Laue, Abraham
Belov 70; Correspondence on Publication of Paper with Bienenstock
Creation of Journal of Applied Crystallography
Bell Telephone Labs talk; silhouettes of Bozorth Family
Warren Award - D. P. recommends Gustav Borrmann
Diffracted Intensity and Electrostatic Energy of a Crystal
Ewald on the beginning of IUC
Acta ms. Newkirk and Smoluchowski F182
Rose and Linda Ewald's school record books
Glass negatives and photographs from gathering in Holzhausen, Ammersee, Germany at Paul Peter Erwald's mother's studio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photo of Ott's carborundum structure with H. Mark, K. Herzfeld, L. Brillouin, R.W. James, A.D. Fokker, H. Ott, M. von Laue, C.G. Darwin, W.L. Bragg and I. Waller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also several photos of unidentified people

did.unittitle: Bibliography of Sommerfeld's Papers, and articles and clippings
Einstein Archive, including copies of letters with Einstein and Sommerfeld
Articles and clippings on C. Hermann, Laue death, Niggli, and others
"The Full Costs of Ballistic Missile Defense", edited by Richard Kaufman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In wooden box, organized by subject, with lists in each box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These documents were stored together in a tied bundle labelled Poly photos and memorabilia

Dr. Carl Schleussner publications and documents
Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen publications and reproductions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Background material for talk at Oxford.

did.unittitle: W.H. Bragg, science and faith publication
Exhibit materials for celebration, FRS - Brooklyn (with Linda present)
Early works, newspaper articles, comments on aviation
